title: Majority Element
prompt:
Given an array `nums` of size `n`, return the majority element.

The majority element is the element that appears more than `n / 2` times. You may assume that the majority element always exists in the array.

examples.output:
2
Explanation: 2 appears 4 times out of 7 elements (4 > 7/2).

constraints: The majority element always exists in the array.

notesForAI.coreIdea: Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm: maintain a candidate and count. For each element, if count is 0, set current as candidate. If current matches candidate, increment count, else decrement. The majority element survives because it has more than half the votes.
commonBugs: Using extra space with a hash map when O(1) space is possible
Not understanding why Boyer-Moore works and implementing it incorrectly
Resetting candidate at the wrong time
